title: What is a Man to Do?
body:
What is a man to do—
born to be strong, to stand tall,
to swallow storms and smile?

Don't cry, they say.
But what of the tears
clinging to the edge of my eyes,
waiting for permission to fall?

Men once fought wars—
they still do,
only now the battlefield
is within.

What is a man to do
when it's 3 a.m.
and the thoughts
are pounding at the door,
begging to be let in?

The war is over.
Or maybe
I'm the one who fell.

What is a man to do?
